name: City Employee Payroll (Current)

columns.name: ROW_ID
columns.description: ONLY used by the Data Owner for Data uploading purpose.

columns.name: JOB_STATUS
columns.description: Employee's job status at the time the data was uploaded

columns.name: RECORD_NBR
columns.description: Unique number to identify an employee

columns.name: EMPLOYMENT_TYPE
columns.description: Employment Type - Full Time, Part Time, or Per Event

columns.name: OVERTIME_PAY
columns.description: Payments attributable to hours worked beyond regular work schedule

columns.name: ALL_OTHER_PAY
columns.description: Any payments other than Regular and Overtime. This includes bonuses, adjustments, and lump sum payouts. Examples of bonuses include Permanent, Longevity, and Temporary Bonuses. Lump Sum Pay includes significant one-time payouts due to retirement, lawsuit settlements, or other adjustments.

columns.name: PAY_YEAR
columns.description: Tax year employee was paid. This is not Fiscal Year.

columns.name: TOTAL_PAY
columns.description: Sum of regular, overtime and all other payments

columns.name: BENEFIT_PAY
columns.description: City contribution for the employee's health care, dental care, vision care, and life insurance

columns.name: DEPARTMENT_NO
columns.description: Department Number in City Payroll System

description: Payroll information for all Los Angeles City Employees including the City's three proprietary departments: Water and Power, Airports and Harbor. Data is updated bi-weekly by the Los Angeles City Controller's Office. Payroll information for employees of the Department of Water and Power is updated every three months.

name: All City Funds

columns.name: Collected Revenue
columns.description: Revenue collected in the current fiscal year

columns.name: Cash Expenses
columns.description: Cash disbursed in the current fiscal year

columns.name: Other Cash Changes
columns.description: Net of other transactions that increase or decrease the cash balance

columns.name: Assets
columns.description: (A) Total Assets include Cash, Grant Receivables, and Other Assets

columns.name: Fund Balance
columns.description: (C) where (C=A-B) - Total Assets minus Total Liabilities

columns.name: Overhead Costs Reimbursement Eligibility
columns.description: Fund is subject to overhead reimbursement costs

columns.name: Grant Project
columns.description: Is the fund associated with a grant?

columns.name: Council File Number
columns.description: City Council file number authorizing the fund

columns.name: Inactive Fund - Close
columns.description: Is this an inactive fund? Default is no

columns.name: Adopted Budget
columns.description: Budget adopted by the Mayor and City Council

columns.name: Capital Project
columns.description: Does this Fund have any Capital Projects?

columns.name: Function
columns.description: Arts/Culture/Tourism, Debt Service, Economic Development, Sanitation & Environment, Other, Housing & Homelessness, Parks, Public Safety, Public Works, Social Services, Streets, or Transportation

columns.name: Beginning Cash
columns.description: Beginning cash balance for the fiscal year

columns.name: Budget Amendments
columns.description: Amendments to the budget adopted by the Mayor and City Council

columns.name: Current Cash Balance
columns.description: Cash Balance at the time of Data update.

columns.name: Fund Purpose
columns.description: Purpose and intent of the fund

columns.name: Fund Category
columns.description: General Fund, Special Fund, or Proprietary

columns.name: Fund Type
columns.description: Agency, Capital Projects, Debt Service, Enterprise, General Fund, Pension Trust, General Long Term Debt Group, or Special Revenue

columns.name: Ordinance Number
columns.description: Ordinance number used to establish the fund

columns.name: Replacement Of General Funds Allowed
columns.description: Replacement of General Fund is allowed

columns.name: Interest To General Fund
columns.description: Interest eligible for the General Fund?

columns.name: General Fund Reversion
columns.description: Is the fund subject to General Fund reversion?

columns.name: Budget Schedule Number
columns.description: Funds with a schedule number have revenue and expenditure budgets reviewed and approved by the Mayor and City Council. Funds without a schedule number are considered "off-budget" since their budgets are not reviewed and approved by the Mayor and City Council.

columns.name: Budget Schedule Name
columns.description: Name of the budget schedule found in the City's budget

description: Current balances of all Funds for the City of Los Angeles. Includes General Fund, Reserve Fund, Budget Stabilization Fund, and Special Funds. Upated daily, Monday through Friday. For figures pertaining to Fiscal Year 2018, view the "All City Funds - FY18" dataset at https://controllerdata.lacity.org/dataset/All-City-Funds-FY18/9wxw-98tn/data.

name: Checkbook L.A. Data

columns.name: CURRENCY
columns.description: Type of currency used in transaction

description: This dataset contains City spending data and is used to power the Controller's Checkbook L.A application. Data since fiscal year 2018.

name: City Revenue

columns.name: FISCAL YEAR
columns.description: Fiscal year (FY) is a period that a company or government uses for accounting purposes and preparing financial statements.

columns.name: DEPARTMENT NAME
columns.description: Name of a City Department, Bureau or Office in the City of Los Angeles.

description: City revenue including adopted, amended, budgeted and revenue collected since fiscal year 2012. Data is updated monthly.

name: City Employee Payroll (2013-2018)

columns.name: Lump Sum Pay
columns.description: Lump sum payouts for special purposes - retirement payouts, back pay, etc.; typically not pensionable

columns.name: Other Pay & Adjustments
columns.description: Payments based on other pay codes or adjustments that do not fall into another category

columns.name: Other Pay (Payroll Explorer)
columns.description: Other Pay includes bonuses, adjustments, and lump sum payouts. Examples of bonuses include Permanent, Longevity, and Temporary Bonuses. Lump Sum Pay includes significant one-time payouts due to retirement, lawsuit settlements, or other adjustments

columns.name: FMS Department

columns.description: Average cost to the City to provide dental care to the employee

columns.name: Average Basic Life
columns.description: Average cost to the City to provide basic life insurance to the employee

columns.name: Average Benefit Cost
columns.description: The total average City contribution for the employee's health care, dental care and life insurance

columns.name: Hourly or Event Rate
columns.description: Hourly Earnings Rate or Per Event Rate based on Projected Annual Salary

columns.name: Projected Annual Salary
columns.description: Budgeted pay amount. Used for pension contribution calculations

columns.name: Q1 Payments
columns.description: Payments for the first quarter of the year from January 1 to March 31

columns.name: Q2 Payments
columns.description: Payments for the second quarter of the year from April 1 to June 30

columns.name: Q3 Payments
columns.description: Payments for the third quarter of the year from July 1 to September 30

columns.name: Q4 Payments
columns.description: Payments for the fourth quarter of the year from October 1 to December 31

columns.description: Payments in excess of Base Pay which may include bonuses and other payouts

columns.name: % Over Base Pay
columns.description: Percentage of payment in excess of Base Pay which may include bonuses and other payouts

columns.name: Longevity Bonus Pay
columns.description: Payments attributable to years of service; typically pensionable

columns.name: Temporary Bonus Pay
columns.description: Payments attributable to temporary bonuses; typically not pensionable

columns.name: Overtime Pay
columns.description: Payments attributable to hours worked beyond regular work schedule

columns.description: Click this hyperlink to view the job class description

columns.name: Average Health Cost
columns.description: Average cost to the City to provide health care to the employee

columns.name: Employment Type
columns.description: Employment Type - Full Time, Part Time, or Per Event

columns.name: Permanent Bonus Pay
columns.description: Payments attributable to permanent bonuses; typically pensionable

columns.name: Pay Grade
columns.description: Pay Grade for the Job Class

columns.name: Payroll Department
columns.description: Department Number in City Payroll System

name: HOMELESSNESS EXPENSE TRACKER
description: City expenditures related to Homelessness efforts in the City of LA starting with fiscal year 2024. Data populated by City departments who code and map expenses to homeless project categories. (Not all departments are included due to the way the department maps homeless expenditures)

name: Idle Funds
provider: City of Los Angeles - Office of the Controller
description: Idle Funds are Los Angeles City funds with no expenditures since June 30, 2014, as identified by LA Controller Ron Galperin.

Pay codes are governed by the Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs) between the City and its labor repres

description: For expenditures prior to July 1, 2014, please visit this dataset: http://controllerdata.lacity.org/resource/f2ec-m4t9 Beginning in 2014, each Neighborhood Council has been given a checking account as part of a new funding model for NC's. This dataset contains the information about each Neighborhood Council's expenditures from those checking accounts. For more information, see the User Guide here: http://empowerla.org/wp-content/uploads/2012/10/NC-Checking-Account-Core-Guide-7-1-14-1.pdf

The Category field indicates which column of the DWP Payroll dataset (https://controllerdata.lacity.org/Payroll/DWP-Payroll/fq29-mxsy) that particular pay code relates to. Pay codes are governed by the Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs) between the DWP and its labor representatives; State, City and Federal law; and Departmen

name: Financial Documents and Reports for the Department of Water and Power
description: A collection of financial documents, performance reports, and independent reviews of the Department of Water and Power (DWP).

name: Appropriations, Expenditures and Revenues
description: Ledger transactions for the City's appropriations, expenditures and revenues are shown in this dataset.

name: Publicly-Owned Properties
description: This dataset contains all properties in the City of Los Angeles owned by the following public entities: Federal GSD, State of California, Los Angeles County, City of Los Angeles, LAUSD, and Metro. This data on property ownership was derived from the data made available by the Los Angeles County Assessor's Office, here: https://data.lacounty.gov/Parcel-/Assessor-Publicly-Owned-Parcels-Listing/a9jw-tqfp/data

name: Economy Panel LA - Data
description: EconomyPanel features demographic and economic data about the City’s fifteen council districts, broken down into six broad categories including: population, gender, income,employment, housing, and building permits.

name: Food Resources in California
description: This dataset contains a list of resources such food pantries, food banks, and other food distribution sites throughout the state of California. The data is current as of May 1, 2020. Due to the coronavirus pandemic, many food pantries may change their hours or close temporarily. Please contact a food pantry prior to visiting them to confirm their operating hours and ensure you are in their service area.

name: City Employee Locations By Department

columns.name: Zip Codes
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'Zip Codes' (b5dh-k22n) the point in column 'location_1' is located. This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

description: A listing of the number of City employees in each zip code, by Department. Data current as of December 31, 2013, and reflects addresses provided by employees to the City's Payroll system. Employee-entered zip codes flagged as errors are set by default to coordinates (39.52368635500045, -99.11415045099966).

name: Joint Training Institute (JTI) and Joint Safety Institute (JSI) Credit Card Expenditures
description: Credit card statements and transaction details for the Joint Safety Institute and the Joint Training Institute. Financial documents and underlying data relating to City Controller Ron Galperin's audits of two trusts, the Joint Training Institute and the Joint Safety Institute. The two trusts are fully funded by the Department of Water and Power (DWP) and managed by an appointed board made up of equal numbers of representatives from DWP management and from the International Brotherhood of Electri

name: DWP Audits
description: A database of audits completed by the Internal Audit section of the Department of Water and Power (DWP). These audits are not validated or verified by the Controller's Internal Audit Division, and represent the work of auditors directly employed by the DWP.

name: City Owned Parcels (2015)
description: Parcels owned by the City of Los Angeles. Data provided by the Los Angeles County Assessor's Office.

name: March 1 Revenue Forecast Report 2019
description: The Revenue Forecast Report for the City of Los Angeles provides a current and updated revenue estimate for fiscal year 2017-18 and revenue projections for fiscal year 2018-19. Also included are the estimated requirements for debt service and General Fund cash flow borrowing. The Forecast is issued pursuant to the Controller’s City Charter mandate to monitor and report on all matters relating to the City’s fiscal health, to keep the City’s official financial records, and to supervise expenditure

name: LADWP Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs)
description: Listing of the Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs) between the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power and the various unions which represent its workers.

columns.description: Fiscal year starts from the July 1st of that calendar year, to the June 30th of next year

columns.description: Batch-generated ID (using base64-encoded timestamp) to uniquely identify each row.

columns.name: Amount
columns.description: The amount made to outside vendors and/or other city departments. A negative amount typically indicates a discount applied to another purchase.

description: Notes: Transactions with a Budget Fiscal Year prior to 2023 are not included (due to limitations in the FMS_GL_09 universe), regardless of the Fiscal Year specified. To ensure data completeness, the bi-weekly update retrieves records based on a record date set two weeks prior to the extraction date, rather than the retrieval date itself.

name: Neighborhood Council Monthly Expenditure Report For Fiscal Year 2021-Current

columns.name: Status
columns.description: There are 4 types of status for a check: 1) "Active" means the check is still outstanding; 2) "Reconciled / Paid Check" indicates the check has been cleared by the bank; 3) "Reissued" means the check may be lost in the mail and not received by the vendor. The neighborhood council reissued a new check. The check marked reissued normally voided; And 4) "Void" states the check is cancelled. Usually credit card transactions would not have a status, and therefore labeled as "N/A" or being blank.

columns.name: Row Id
columns.description: unique identifier of each transaction that is made up with transaction year, month and four-digit batch id

columns.name: Type of Payment
columns.description: Credit card transaction is billed as "Electronic" and the "Check" is issued by check

name: Year Start Reserve Fund Balances

columns.name: % Total Reserve Fund to Budgeted General Fund Receipts
Less: Transfer to Budget Stabilization Fund

description: Shows the balance of the City's Reserve Fund, including transfers in and transfers out, beginning in Fiscal Year 1998-1999. The Reserve Fund is an important part of the City's financial health, and is relied on in the event of a natural disaster or other crisis situation. The City's current policy is to maintain a minimum Reserve Fund balance of 5% of General Fund receipts.

description: Housing and Community Investment Department's (HCID) monitoring data from 2014. HCID’s inventory does not include all of the affordable units administered by the Housing Authority of the City of Los Angeles (HACLA) - HACLA’s portfolio includes 79,379 units, which consist of Public Housing Units, Housing Choice Vouchers, Project Based Section 8 properties and other housing assistance program units. Source: HACLA’s 2016 Final Agency Plan.

columns.name: Activity Type
columns.description: Governmental Activities are functions principally supported by taxes and intergovernmental revenues. Business-Type Activities are functions intended to recover all (or in large part) costs through user fees and charges. Primary Government is a combination of Governmental and Business-Type Activities.

columns.name: Fiscal Year
columns.description: Fiscal Year (FY) is the time period for the City budget which begins on July 1 of a calendar year and ends on June 30 of the following year.

columns.name: Asset Type
columns.description: Type of asset the City owns

columns.name: Amount in Thousands
columns.description: Net Position (Assets minus Liabilities) in Thousands of Dollars

description: CAFR Pages 330-331. Net Position is the difference between the City’s assets and liabilities, including deferred outflows/inflows of resources. This data summarizes Net Position by Activity Types, Asset Type Groups, and Asset Types. This data is based on the following parameters: Accrual Basis of Accounting, Last Ten Fiscal Years and (amounts expressed in thousands).

name: Council Districts Discretionary Balance Since FY23

name: Every Animal Counts
description: City Controller Ron Galperin released an audit that indicated the Los Angeles’ Department of Animal Services is making progress toward its goal of making Los Angeles a “No Kill” City. Still, last year about one of every four lost or abandoned animals that entered the City’s shelters were euthanized. View the department's raw data on each of the City's six animal shelters below.

name: Small Business Emergency Microloans
description: The City of Los Angeles created the Small Business Emergency Microloan Program in March 2020 to distribute $11 million to local businesses that are not eligible for federal CARES Act funds. L.A. Controller Ron Galperin, in coordination with Economic and Workforce Development Department (EWDD), created a Microloan Tracker to ensure public accountability and give Angelenos access to up-to-date information on how the City is supporting the local economy. Use this dataset to find microloan recipient

name: Salary Rate for Elected Officials
description: Salary rate for elected officials is determined per Charter Section 218, which states that "a Council Member's salary is equal to salary prescribed by law for judges of Municipal Court of LA Judicial District or its successor." It further stipulates that the City Controller gets 10% more than Council Member (CM), City Attorney gets 20% more than CM and the Mayor gets 30% more than CM.
